subject: Chemistry
title: Distillation: Alchemy's Greatest Tool
description: A quick, practical guide to Distillation: Alchemy's Greatest Tool.

hook.text: Distillation separates liquids by boiling point. It gave alchemists pure acids, alcohols, and essential oils.

buildup.text: An alembic heats a liquid until it vaporizes. The vapor travels through a cooled tube and condenses as a purer liquid.

discovery.text: Islamic alchemists perfected this technique. The word 'alcohol' comes from Arabic 'al-kuhl'—a distilled substance.

twist.text: Distillation let them isolate nitric acid, sulfuric acid, and ethanol—three reagents that revolutionized chemistry.

climax.text: Today, distillation refines crude oil into gasoline, diesel, and jet fuel. The same principle, industrial scale.

punchline.text: Heat, evaporate, condense. One technique that powers chemistry and industry.

quiz.question: What Arabic word is the origin of 'alcohol'?
